default_100pt_rubric.title: Default 100-Point Rubric
default_100pt_rubric.description: General purpose rubric for programming assignments

criteria.name: Understanding & Correctness
criteria.description: Demonstrates understanding of concepts and correct implementation

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Complete understanding with excellent implementation; all requirements met with sophisticated approaches

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Good understanding with correct implementation; most requirements met with solid approaches

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Partial understanding with some correct implementation; some requirements met but gaps remain

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: Limited understanding; minimal correct implementation; many requirements unmet

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: All requirements fully met; includes enhancements beyond requirements

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: All major requirements met; minor requirements may have small gaps

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Most requirements addressed but significant gaps in coverage

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: Many requirements missing or inadequately addressed

criteria.name: Code Quality / Clarity
criteria.description: Code is clear, well-structured, and maintainable

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Exceptionally clear and well-structured; uses best practices consistently

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Clear and well-structured with minor areas for improvement

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Readable but has structural issues or unclear sections

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: Difficult to understand; poor structure or organization

criteria.name: Style / Conventions
criteria.description: Follows language conventions and coding standards

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Consistently follows all conventions and standards

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Follows most conventions with minor inconsistencies

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Follows some conventions but has noticeable style issues

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: Does not follow conventions; inconsistent or poor style

csc151_java_exam_rubric.title: CSC 151 Java Exam Rubric (Brightspace-aligned)
csc151_java_exam_rubric.description: Aligned to the official Brightspace Exam Grading Rubric for CSC151. Program Performance is scored strictly by error counts. Rule: Every 4 Minor Errors convert into 1 Major Error before scoring.

criteria.name: Program Performance
criteria.description: Apply Minor→Major conversion first. Every 4 Minor Errors become 1 Major Error (remainder stays Minor). Then select the score band.

levels.label: A+ (0 errors)
levels.description: Perfect - no errors detected

levels.label: A (1 minor error)
levels.description: Excellent - only 1 minor error

levels.label: A- (2 minor errors)
levels.description: Very good - 2 minor errors

levels.label: B (3 minor errors)
levels.description: Good - 3 minor errors

levels.label: B- (1 major error)
levels.description: Satisfactory - 1 major error

levels.label: C (2 major errors)
levels.description: Acceptable - 2 major errors

levels.label: D (3 major errors)
levels.description: Poor - 3 major errors

levels.label: F (4+ major errors)
levels.description: Failing - 4 or more major errors

levels.label: 0 (Not submitted or incomplete)
levels.description: Not submitted or incomplete

ai_assignment_reflection_rubric.title: AI Assignment Reflection Rubric
ai_assignment_reflection_rubric.description: Rubric for grading AI tool reflection assignments. Uses level-band scoring where LLM selects performance levels and backend computes exact points.

criteria.name: Tool Description & Usage
criteria.description: Demonstrates clear understanding of the AI tool's purpose, features, and how it was used in the assignment

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Comprehensive description of the AI tool with detailed explanation of features used; demonstrates sophisticated understanding of tool capabilities

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Clear description of the AI tool and its main features; explains how the tool was used effectively

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Basic description of the AI tool; mentions some features but lacks detail on usage or effectiveness

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: Minimal or unclear description of the AI tool; does not adequately explain usage or features

criteria.name: Intelligence Analysis
criteria.description: Analyzes the intelligence and limitations of the AI tool; provides thoughtful reflection on its capabilities and constraints

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Insightful and nuanced analysis of AI intelligence; identifies both capabilities and limitations with specific examples; demonstrates critical thinking

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Good analysis of AI intelligence; identifies key capabilities and some limitations; provides relevant examples

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Basic analysis of AI intelligence; mentions capabilities or limitations but lacks depth; few or generic examples

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: Superficial or missing analysis; does not adequately explore AI capabilities or limitations

criteria.name: Personal Goals & Application
criteria.description: Connects AI tool usage to personal learning goals and future applications; reflects on learning and growth

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Thoughtful connection between AI tool and personal goals; detailed reflection on learning; specific plans for future application

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Clear connection to personal goals; reflects on learning experience; mentions future applications

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Basic connection to goals; limited reflection; vague about future applications

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: Minimal or no connection to personal goals; lacks reflection or future application plans

criteria.name: Presentation & Requirements
criteria.description: Meets assignment requirements including format, length, organization, and writing quality

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Exceeds all requirements; well-organized; excellent writing quality; professional presentation

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Meets all requirements; good organization; clear writing; appropriate presentation

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Meets most requirements; some organizational or writing issues; adequate presentation

levels.label: Beginning
levels.description: Missing requirements; poor organization or writing quality; inadequate presentation
